creator: direct727@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to David Chisnall from comment #2)
> The assembler errors look like they're caused by GNU binutils problems. 
> Does adding -fintegrated-as to the CFLAGS fix it for you?

I tried this flag... (and I couldn't find anything close to it on Google to try instead).

# export CFLAGS=-fintegrated-as
# cd /usr/ports/lang/libobjc2/
# make clean
...
# make
# make 
===>   libobjc2-1.7_1 depends on file: /usr/local/bin/cmake - found
===>  Configuring for libobjc2-1.7_1
===>  Performing in-source build
/bin/mkdir -p /usr/ports/lang/libobjc2/work/libobjc2-1.7
-- The C compiler identification is unknown
-- The CXX compiler identification is unknown
-- Check for working C compiler: /usr/bin/clang
-- Check for working C compiler: /usr/bin/clang -- broken
CMake Error at /usr/local/share/cmake/Modules/CMakeTestCCompiler.cmake:61 (message):

  program.

  It fails with the following output:
